content.content:
1	{"apiVersion":"tideproject.k8s/v1alpha1","kind":"TaskEnvelopeIn","taskUID":"df5a0c1f-a6c5-4a8a-a56f-4a24c7a56eab","role":"planner","level":"project","prompt":"## Outcome

Add a second Subagent implementation for the Codex runtime (OpenAI) and
demonstrate real heterogeneous dispatch — mixed-provider waves that actually
run, not just interface scaffolding. Concrete outcome: a TIDE operator can
configure a Project where the planner pool runs Claude and the executor pool
runs Codex, and TIDE dispatches a multi-phase run to completion with correct
wave-boundary failure semantics across both providers.

## Current foundation — ALREADY IN MAIN, build ON it, do NOT rebuild

Much of what early drafts of this run assumed it would build now ships in
main (milestone v1.0.2 "Spring Tide" + late v1.0.1). Treat all of this as
given infrastructure; the ONLY new product surface this run delivers is the
Codex subagent package + the per-level provider switch.

- **v1alpha2 schema + global Execution DAG (Phases 23-26).** `Plan`/`Phase`/
  `Milestone`/`Task` all carry `dependsOn` at any level; the assembler builds
  ONE global Execution DAG and derives Project-scoped waves
  (`tide-wave-<project>-<N>`) across plan/phase/milestone boundaries via
  layered Kahn. The project planner emits N Milestones up-front with
  `Milestone.dependsOn` (planning-DAG edge only — zero execution edges).
  Child CRDs authored by the planner are v1alpha2.
- **Global dispatch / failure / gates / resumption (Phase 25).** Readiness is
  re-derived from the global indegree map + completed-set each reconcile
  (nothing cached). `failureProfile` and the gate-as-hold model below are live.
- **Cost / budget / telemetry / cache (Phases 16, 18-21).** `pkg/dispatch.Usage`,
  `tide_cost_cents_total`, budget enforcement, and the dashboard Telemetry tab
  + cache-efficiency panel all exist. The locked metric label set is
  {project,phase,plan,wave}; the `task` label is FORBIDDEN (cardinality analyzer).
- **Dashboard (Phase 26).** A global execution-DAG view renders the whole-Project
  wave DAG (dagre LR); run #2's waves/spend are watchable live.

## Architecture — locked decisions (do NOT reinvent these)

**Implementation location: internal/subagent/codex/**
Mirror the existing internal/subagent/anthropic/ layering exactly:
- internal/subagent/codex/client.go — Client struct + constructor
- internal/subagent/codex/run.go — Subagent.Run(ctx, EnvelopeIn) (EnvelopeOut, error)
- internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile — multi-stage build mirroring anthropic/Dockerfile
- internal/subagent/codex/doc.go — package doc referencing D-C1 layering pattern

All OpenAI/Codex-specific code stays behind the pkg/dispatch.Subagent
interface. No provider hard-coding in the controller. No Codex imports
in internal/controller/ or cmd/manager/.

**Per-level runtime selection (not per-task, not per-project).**
Planner pool and executor pool each pin a runtime. This is the correct
granularity: it matches the spec's separate-pools argument, achieves real
heterogeneity within a single run, and avoids the API/scheduler complexity
of per-task selection. Rejected alternatives (do not reopen):
- Per-task selection: large API surface, non-trivial scheduler changes.
- Per-project selection: no mixing within a run (defeats heterogeneity goal).

The SubagentConfig field already has Levels.{Milestone,Phase,Plan,Task}
per-level LevelConfig overrides (api/v1alpha2/project_types.go via
SubagentConfig). Wire the Codex image at the levels the operator wants Codex.
NOTE: today `resolveImage` honors a per-level image, but `ResolveProvider`
(internal/controller/dispatch_helpers.go) still returns Vendor:"anthropic"
unconditionally — so a non-Anthropic image at a level is selected but then
rejected at runtime (the image's vendor sentinel expects an anthropic
envelope). Adding the per-level vendor/provider switch IS part of this run's
deliverable.

**Credential path.**
OPENAI_API_KEY or CODEX_API_KEY via a K8s Secret — the established
Secret-ref pattern from providerSecretRef. Never mount host config, never
headless OAuth (broken: claude-code#29983, #7100 — same restriction applies
to Codex). Chart values for the new Secret name are part of the deliverable.

**Mixed-provider waves: identical wave-boundary failure semantics.**
The spec's failure handling (spec section "Failure handling at wave
boundaries") applies unchanged regardless of which provider runs a task:
- Failed task: siblings in the same wave continue (they were declared
  independent).
- Dependents in later waves: never dispatch.
- Non-dependents in later waves: dispatch in strict profile.
This contract must not change when tasks run on different providers. It is now
enforced by the global indegree model (Phase 25); do not add a parallel path.

**Usage accounting + cost — REQUIRED for budget + observability.**
The cost/budget/telemetry surfaces (Phases 16, 18-21) depend on every Subagent
populating pkg/dispatch.Usage. The Codex Run() MUST normalize the OpenAI/Codex
usage block into that struct, or budget enforcement, tide_cost_cents_total, AND
the cache-efficiency dashboard panel will all read $0 / blank for Codex:
- InputTokens        <- usage.prompt_tokens
- OutputTokens       <- usage.completion_tokens
- CacheReadTokens    <- usage.prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens
  (OpenAI caching is AUTOMATIC, marker-free, process-independent, 1,024-token
  floor — there is NO cache_control to set and NO separate "cache creation"
  concept, so CacheCreationTokens stays 0. This differs from Anthropic, whose
  field is cache_read_input_tokens / cache_creation_input_tokens.)
- EstimatedCostCents + CacheSavingsCents <- computed by a Codex-side pricing
  table mirroring internal/subagent/anthropic/pricing.go (per-model cents/MTok;
  cacheRead is OpenAI's discounted cached-input rate). Without this table the
  run cannot honor its own budget cap and the dashboard cannot attribute Codex
  spend. Keep the locked metric label set {project,phase,plan,wave} — the
  `task` label remains FORBIDDEN (metriccardinality analyzer).
The provider-neutral SharedContext field (Phase 20) on EnvelopeIn is shared
Go struct state — the Codex subagent receives it for free; no special handling.

**Codex CLI facts — carry verbatim, do NOT re-research:**
- codex exec is first-class headless: one-shot, no TTY, final message
  to stdout.
- Flags for clean containers: --ephemeral, --skip-git-repo-check,
  --ignore-user-config.
- --json emits a JSONL event stream.
- --output-schema <file> constrains the final response to a JSON schema
  — use this for child-CRD emission (child-CRD format is JSON, mandated).
- Sandbox defaults read-only; the runner needs --sandbox workspace-write
  (fine inside an already-isolated pod — mirrors the Claude runner posture).
- API-key env auth (OPENAI_API_KEY) is the documented container path —
  no headless-OAuth problem.
- MCP client support (STDIO + streamable HTTP) via config.toml or
  codex mcp add — available if needed for child-CRD emission.
- Sources: developers.openai.com/codex/{noninteractive,cli/reference,
  auth,cli/features} (verified 2026-06).

**Container image.**
internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile must build clean. The final image
bundles the Codex CLI binary. Image reference convention mirrors
ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-claude-subagent (replace "claude" with
"codex" in the image name; exact tag/registry are authoring decisions).

## Constraints

- All Codex/OpenAI-specific code stays in internal/subagent/codex/ and
  behind the pkg/dispatch.Subagent interface — no provider leakage into
  the controller or cmd/manager.
- Wave-boundary failure semantics unchanged for mixed-provider waves.
- Secret material never inlined in manifests or chart values — reference
  by Secret name only.
- Follow all CLAUDE.md conventions: Apache 2.0 headers, logr/zap logging,
  controller-runtime patterns; keep make verify-* guards green.
- Do NOT break existing tests. Confirm make test is green before
  authoring any plan as delivered.
- Do NOT add a real OpenAI API call in tests without an explicit test-env
  guard (OPENAI_API_KEY env set) — tests must pass offline.
","filesTouched":null,"declaredOutputPaths":null,"caps":{"wallClockSeconds":1800,"iterations":20,"inputTokens":0,"outputTokens":0},"proxyEndpoint":"https://127.0.0.1:8443","signedToken":"","provider":{"vendor":"anthropic","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6"},"dispatch":{"parentName":"dogfood-codex-runtime"}}

content.content:
1	# Project: dogfood-codex-runtime — Milestone Plan
2	
3	**Outcome:** Add a second Subagent implementation for the Codex (OpenAI) runtime and demonstrate real heterogeneous dispatch — mixed-provider waves that actually run, not just interface scaffolding. Gate: a TIDE operator configures a Project where planner pool runs Claude and executor pool runs Codex; TIDE dispatches a multi-phase run to completion with correct wave-boundary failure semantics across both providers.
4	
5	**Foundation (already in main — do NOT rebuild):** v1alpha2 global Execution DAG, layered Kahn wave derivation, global dispatch/failure/gates/resumption, cost/budget/telemetry/cache, and the full dashboard are all live.
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Milestone 01 — Codex Subagent Package
10	
11	**Name:** `milestone-01-codex-subagent`
12	**DependsOn:** _(none — root milestone)_
13	
14	### Outcome
15	
16	A complete, independently testable Go package at `internal/subagent/codex/` that implements `pkg/dispatch.Subagent` against the Codex CLI (OpenAI). The package is provider-firewalled: zero Codex/OpenAI imports leak into `internal/controller/` or `cmd/manager/`. All tests pass offline (`make test` green; any live-API test is guarded by `OPENAI_API_KEY` env).
17	
18	### Deliverables
19	
20	- **`internal/subagent/codex/doc.go`** — package doc; references D-C1 layering pattern and the `vendorSentinel = "openai"` compile-time guard.
21	- **`internal/subagent/codex/client.go`** — `Client` struct + constructor; wraps Codex CLI invocation (`codex exec --ephemeral --skip-git-repo-check --ignore-user-config --json --sandbox workspace-write --output-schema <schema-file>`); reads `OPENAI_API_KEY` from env (mounted from K8s Secret by the dispatch Job spec).
22	- **`internal/subagent/codex/run.go`** — `Subagent.Run(ctx, EnvelopeIn) (EnvelopeOut, error)`. Parses the JSONL event stream from `codex exec --json`; extracts the final message; normalizes OpenAI usage block into `pkg/dispatch.Usage`:
23	  - `InputTokens` ← `usage.prompt_tokens`
24	  - `OutputTokens` ← `usage.completion_tokens`
25	  - `CacheReadTokens` ← `usage.prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens` (OpenAI automatic caching — no `cache_control`, no `CacheCreationTokens`)
26	  - `EstimatedCostCents` + `CacheSavingsCents` ← per-model pricing table (below)
27	- **`internal/subagent/codex/pricing.go`** — per-model cents/MTok table mirroring `internal/subagent/anthropic/pricing.go`. Covers at minimum `codex-1` / `o4-mini` / `o3` / `gpt-4.1`. `cacheRead` column uses OpenAI's 50%-off cached-input rate.
28	- **`internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile`** — multi-stage build mirroring `internal/subagent/anthropic/Dockerfile`; final image installs the Codex CLI binary; image name convention `ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-codex-subagent`.
29	- **Unit tests** — `run_test.go`, `pricing_test.go`; all offline-safe; any test needing a real API call gated by `if os.Getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") == ""`.
30	
31	### Exit Criteria
32	
33	1. `make test` green with zero new failures.
34	2. `make verify-*` guards green (Apache 2.0 headers, provider-firewall analyzer passes — no Codex imports in controller or manager).
35	3. `internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile` builds a runnable image (`docker build` exits 0).
36	4. `Subagent.Run` correctly rejects an envelope whose `Provider.Vendor != "openai"` (vendor sentinel test).
37	5. Pricing table round-trips: given known prompt/completion/cache token counts, `EstimatedCostCents` matches expected value within floating-point tolerance.
38	
39	---
40	
41	## Milestone 02 — Per-Level Provider Dispatch
42	
43	**Name:** `milestone-02-provider-dispatch`
44	**DependsOn:** `milestone-01-codex-subagent`
45	
46	### Outcome
47	
48	The TIDE controller dispatches subagent Jobs using the vendor declared in the per-level image config — not the hardcoded `"anthropic"` string that `ResolveProvider` currently returns unconditionally. An operator setting a Codex image at `spec.subagent.levels.task.image` (or an equivalent per-level vendor field) gets a Job whose `EnvelopeIn.Provider.Vendor == "openai"`, and the Codex subagent image accepts it. Helm chart gains the Codex image ref and a second provider Secret name. `make test` stays green.
49	
50	### Deliverables
51	
52	- **`api/v1alpha1/project_types.go` — `LevelConfig` extension:** Add `Vendor string` field (or a documented convention that vendor is inferred from the image name when the image matches a known sentinel prefix). Either approach must be locked and justified in the PLAN; no reopening mid-implementation.
53	- **`internal/controller/dispatch_helpers.go` — `ResolveProvider` rewrite:** Walk the same precedence chain as `resolveImage` (`levels.<level>.vendor` → `spec.subagent.vendor` → Helm default) and populate `ProviderSpec.Vendor` from config rather than hardcoding `"anthropic"`. The model precedence chain is unchanged.
54	- **`api/v1alpha1/project_types.go` — `SecretRefs` extension:** Add `OpenAIAPIKey string` field (mirroring `AnthropicAPIKey`) so operators declare the Secret name carrying `OPENAI_API_KEY`; the dispatch Job spec mounts it into the subagent container's env.
55	- **`internal/controller/reporter_jobspec.go` (or equivalent):** Wire the per-level secret ref into the Job pod spec so Codex-dispatched pods receive `OPENAI_API_KEY` from the declared K8s Secret — never from a host-config mount, never inlined.
56	- **`charts/tide/values.yaml`:** Add `images.codexSubagent.*` block (repository, tag, pullPolicy) and `subagent.levels.task.*` defaults showing the Codex image and vendor. Add comment block for the new `secretRefs.openAIAPIKey` operator knob.
57	- **`zz_generated.deepcopy.go`** regenerated if `LevelConfig` or `SecretRefs` gains fields with pointer/slice/map types.
58	- **Tests:** Update `dispatch_helpers_test.go` and `dispatch_image_test.go` to cover the new per-level vendor resolution path; cover both `"anthropic"` and `"openai"` resolution branches.
59	
60	### Exit Criteria
61	
62	1. `make test` green; no new failures.
63	2. `ResolveProvider` with a project whose `spec.subagent.levels.task` declares vendor `"openai"` returns `ProviderSpec{Vendor: "openai", ...}` — asserted in unit tests.
64	3. `ResolveProvider` with no per-level override continues to return `Vendor: "anthropic"` (backward-compat regression test).
65	4. `make verify-*` guards green; no Codex imports in controller or manager.
66	5. `api/v1alpha1/zz_generated.deepcopy.go` is up to date (`make generate` idempotent).
67	6. Helm chart `helm lint` passes with the new values block.
68	
69	---
70	
71	## Milestone 03 — Heterogeneous Integration
72	
73	**Name:** `milestone-03-hetero-integration`
74	**DependsOn:** `milestone-02-provider-dispatch`
75	
76	### Outcome
77	
78	An operator can apply a single Project CR that routes milestone/phase/plan levels to Claude and the task level to Codex; TIDE dispatches the run to completion with waves spanning both providers and correct wave-boundary failure semantics throughout. This milestone is the proof-of-concept that every prior deliverable is wired end-to-end.
79	
80	### Deliverables
81	
82	- **`examples/projects/hetero-codex/project.yaml`** — a minimal but real Project CR demonstrating the mixed-provider configuration:
83	  - `spec.subagent.image` set to the Claude image (planner default).
84	  - `spec.subagent.levels.task.image` and `.vendor` set to the Codex image/`"openai"`.
85	  - `spec.secretRefs.anthropicAPIKey` and `spec.secretRefs.openAIAPIKey` referencing separate K8s Secrets (no credential inlining).
86	  - `spec.subagent.levels.task.model` set to a Codex model identifier (e.g. `codex-1`).
87	  - At least two phases with a cross-phase `dependsOn` edge to exercise a wave boundary.
88	- **`docs/hetero-providers.md`** — operator runbook: how to create the two Secrets, apply the Project CR, watch the global DAG on the dashboard, and interpret wave-boundary failure events across providers.
89	- **Integration test / dogfood verification:** Either a `make test-int` target (guarded by `OPENAI_API_KEY` + `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` env) that runs the hetero project against a live kind cluster, OR a recorded trace (wave log + cost telemetry snapshot) demonstrating mixed-provider dispatch. The test or trace must show:
90	  1. Wave N tasks dispatched to Claude subagent pods — `EnvelopeIn.Provider.Vendor == "anthropic"`.
91	  2. Wave M tasks (task-level, post-planning) dispatched to Codex subagent pods — `EnvelopeIn.Provider.Vendor == "openai"`.
92	  3. A simulated task failure in a Codex-dispatched wave does not abort sibling tasks in the same wave and correctly blocks only dependent waves — not independent later waves (wave-boundary failure semantics, unchanged by provider mix).
93	  4. `tide_cost_cents_total` emits non-zero values for both `vendor=anthropic` and `vendor=openai` label combinations, and the dashboard's cache-efficiency panel shows Codex cache-read savings.
94	- **`charts/tide/values.yaml` or example overlay:** A minimal Helm override snippet (`--set` one-liner or `values-hetero.yaml`) showing how to configure both provider images and both Secret refs in one install.
95	
96	### Exit Criteria
97	
98	1. `make test` green; no regressions.
99	2. Example Project CR passes `kubectl apply --dry-run=client` (schema validation) against the live CRDs.
100	3. Integration test or recorded trace documents steps 1–4 from the deliverable list above.
101	4. `docs/hetero-providers.md` is complete enough for an operator unfamiliar with TIDE internals to execute the mixed-provider setup from scratch.
102	5. Provider-firewall analyzer (`make verify-*`) still reports zero violations — no Codex/OpenAI imports in controller or manager packages after all wiring is complete.
103	
104	---
105	
106	## DAG Summary
107	
108	```
109	milestone-01-codex-subagent   (root — no deps)
110	        |
111	        v
112	milestone-02-provider-dispatch
113	        |
114	        v
115	milestone-03-hetero-integration
116	```
117	
118	**M01** delivers the Codex package in isolation — a reviewable, testable unit behind the `pkg/dispatch.Subagent` interface.
119	**M02** makes the controller provider-neutral — `ResolveProvider` reads config rather than hardcoding `"anthropic"`, Helm chart gains Codex knobs.
120	**M03** closes the loop — a real Project CR dispatches Claude planners + Codex executors across wave boundaries with correct failure semantics and observable cost telemetry for both providers.
121

file.content:
# Project: dogfood-codex-runtime — Milestone Plan

**Outcome:** Add a second Subagent implementation for the Codex (OpenAI) runtime and demonstrate real heterogeneous dispatch — mixed-provider waves that actually run, not just interface scaffolding. Gate: a TIDE operator configures a Project where planner pool runs Claude and executor pool runs Codex; TIDE dispatches a multi-phase run to completion with correct wave-boundary failure semantics across both providers.

**Foundation (already in main — do NOT rebuild):** v1alpha2 global Execution DAG, layered Kahn wave derivation, global dispatch/failure/gates/resumption, cost/budget/telemetry/cache, and the full dashboard are all live.

---

## Milestone 01 — Codex Subagent Package

**Name:** `milestone-01-codex-subagent`
**DependsOn:** _(none — root milestone)_

### Outcome

A complete, independently testable Go package at `internal/subagent/codex/` that implements `pkg/dispatch.Subagent` against the Codex CLI (OpenAI). The package is provider-firewalled: zero Codex/OpenAI imports leak into `internal/controller/` or `cmd/manager/`. All tests pass offline (`make test` green; any live-API test is guarded by `OPENAI_API_KEY` env).

### Deliverables

- **`internal/subagent/codex/doc.go`** — package doc; references D-C1 layering pattern and the `vendorSentinel = "openai"` compile-time guard.
- **`internal/subagent/codex/client.go`** — `Client` struct + constructor; wraps Codex CLI invocation (`codex exec --ephemeral --skip-git-repo-check --ignore-user-config --json --sandbox workspace-write --output-schema <schema-file>`); reads `OPENAI_API_KEY` from env (mounted from K8s Secret by the dispatch Job spec).
- **`internal/subagent/codex/run.go`** — `Subagent.Run(ctx, EnvelopeIn) (EnvelopeOut, error)`. Parses the JSONL event stream from `codex exec --json`; extracts the final message; normalizes OpenAI usage block into `pkg/dispatch.Usage`:
  - `InputTokens` ← `usage.prompt_tokens`
  - `OutputTokens` ← `usage.completion_tokens`
  - `CacheReadTokens` ← `usage.prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens` (OpenAI automatic caching — no `cache_control`, no `CacheCreationTokens`)
  - `EstimatedCostCents` + `CacheSavingsCents` ← per-model pricing table (below)
- **`internal/subagent/codex/pricing.go`** — per-model cents/MTok table mirroring `internal/subagent/anthropic/pricing.go`. Covers at minimum `codex-1` / `o4-mini` / `o3` / `gpt-4.1`. `cacheRead` column uses OpenAI's 50%-off cached-input rate.
- **`internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile`** — multi-stage build mirroring `internal/subagent/anthropic/Dockerfile`; final image installs the Codex CLI binary; image name convention `ghcr.io/jsquirrelz/tide-codex-subagent`.
- **Unit tests** — `run_test.go`, `pricing_test.go`; all offline-safe; any test needing a real API call gated by `if os.Getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY") == ""`.

### Exit Criteria

1. `make test` green with zero new failures.
2. `make verify-*` guards green (Apache 2.0 headers, provider-firewall analyzer passes — no Codex imports in controller or manager).
3. `internal/subagent/codex/Dockerfile` builds a runnable image (`docker build` exits 0).
4. `Subagent.Run` correctly rejects an envelope whose `Provider.Vendor != "openai"` (vendor sentinel test).
5. Pricing table round-trips: given known prompt/completion/cache token counts, `EstimatedCostCents` matches expected value within floating-point tolerance.

---

## Milestone 02 — Per-Level Provider Dispatch

**Name:** `milestone-02-provider-dispatch`
**DependsOn:** `milestone-01-codex-subagent`

### Outcome

The TIDE controller dispatches subagent Jobs using the vendor declared in the per-level image config — not the hardcoded `"anthropic"` string that `ResolveProvider` currently returns unconditionally. An operator setting a Codex image at `spec.subagent.levels.task.image` (or an equivalent per-level vendor field) gets a Job whose `EnvelopeIn.Provider.Vendor == "openai"`, and the Codex subagent image accepts it. Helm chart gains the Codex image ref and a second provider Secret name. `make test` stays green.

### Deliverables

- **`api/v1alpha1/project_types.go` — `LevelConfig` extension:** Add `Vendor string` field (or a documented convention that vendor is inferred from the image name when the image matches a known sentinel prefix). Either approach must be locked and justified in the PLAN; no reopening mid-implementation.
- **`internal/controller/dispatch_helpers.go` — `ResolveProvider` rewrite:** Walk the same precedence chain as `resolveImage` (`levels.<level>.vendor` → `spec.subagent.vendor` → Helm default) and populate `ProviderSpec.Vendor` from config rather than hardcoding `"anthropic"`. The model precedence chain is unchanged.
- **`api/v1alpha1/project_types.go` — `SecretRefs` extension:** Add `OpenAIAPIKey string` field (mirroring `AnthropicAPIKey`) so operators declare the Secret name carrying `OPENAI_API_KEY`; the dispatch Job spec mounts it into the subagent container's env.
- **`internal/controller/reporter_jobspec.go` (or equivalent):** Wire the per-level secret ref into the Job pod spec so Codex-dispatched pods receive `OPENAI_API_KEY` from the declared K8s Secret — never from a host-config mount, never inlined.
- **`charts/tide/values.yaml`:** Add `images.codexSubagent.*` block (repository, tag, pullPolicy) and `subagent.levels.task.*` defaults showing the Codex image and vendor. Add comment block for the new `secretRefs.openAIAPIKey` operator knob.
- **`zz_generated.deepcopy.go`** regenerated if `LevelConfig` or `SecretRefs` gains fields with pointer/slice/map types.
- **Tests:** Update `dispatch_helpers_test.go` and `dispatch_image_test.go` to cover the new per-level vendor resolution path; cover both `"anthropic"` and `"openai"` resolution branches.

### Exit Criteria

1. `make test` green; no new failures.
2. `ResolveProvider` with a project whose `spec.subagent.levels.task` declares vendor `"openai"` returns `ProviderSpec{Vendor: "openai", ...}` — asserted in unit tests.
3. `ResolveProvider` with no per-level override continues to return `Vendor: "anthropic"` (backward-compat regression test).
4. `make verify-*` guards green; no Codex imports in controller or manager.
5. `api/v1alpha1/zz_generated.deepcopy.go` is up to date (`make generate` idempotent).
6. Helm chart `helm lint` passes with the new values block.

---

## Milestone 03 — Heterogeneous Integration

**Name:** `milestone-03-hetero-integration`
**DependsOn:** `milestone-02-provider-dispatch`

### Outcome

An operator can apply a single Project CR that routes milestone/phase/plan levels to Claude and the task level to Codex; TIDE dispatches the run to completion with waves spanning both providers and correct wave-boundary failure semantics throughout. This milestone is the proof-of-concept that every prior deliverable is wired end-to-end.

### Deliverables

- **`examples/projects/hetero-codex/project.yaml`** — a minimal but real Project CR demonstrating the mixed-provider configuration:
  - `spec.subagent.image` set to the Claude image (planner default).
  - `spec.subagent.levels.task.image` and `.vendor` set to the Codex image/`"openai"`.
  - `spec.secretRefs.anthropicAPIKey` and `spec.secretRefs.openAIAPIKey` referencing separate K8s Secrets (no credential inlining).
  - `spec.subagent.levels.task.model` set to a Codex model identifier (e.g. `codex-1`).
  - At least two phases with a cross-phase `dependsOn` edge to exercise a wave boundary.
- **`docs/hetero-providers.md`** — operator runbook: how to create the two Secrets, apply the Project CR, watch the global DAG on the dashboard, and interpret wave-boundary failure events across providers.
- **Integration test / dogfood verification:** Either a `make test-int` target (guarded by `OPENAI_API_KEY` + `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` env) that runs the hetero project against a live kind cluster, OR a recorded trace (wave log + cost telemetry snapshot) demonstrating mixed-provider dispatch. The test or trace must show:
  1. Wave N tasks dispatched to Claude subagent pods — `EnvelopeIn.Provider.Vendor == "anthropic"`.
  2. Wave M tasks (task-level, post-planning) dispatched to Codex subagent pods — `EnvelopeIn.Provider.Vendor == "openai"`.
  3. A simulated task failure in a Codex-dispatched wave does not abort sibling tasks in the same wave and correctly blocks only dependent waves — not independent later waves (wave-boundary failure semantics, unchanged by provider mix).
  4. `tide_cost_cents_total` emits non-zero values for both `vendor=anthropic` and `vendor=openai` label combinations, and the dashboard's cache-efficiency panel shows Codex cache-read savings.
- **`charts/tide/values.yaml` or example overlay:** A minimal Helm override snippet (`--set` one-liner or `values-hetero.yaml`) showing how to configure both provider images and both Secret refs in one install.

### Exit Criteria

1. `make test` green; no regressions.
2. Example Project CR passes `kubectl apply --dry-run=client` (schema validation) against the live CRDs.
3. Integration test or recorded trace documents steps 1–4 from the deliverable list above.
4. `docs/hetero-providers.md` is complete enough for an operator unfamiliar with TIDE internals to execute the mixed-provider setup from scratch.
5. Provider-firewall analyzer (`make verify-*`) still reports zero violations — no Codex/OpenAI imports in controller or manager packages after all wiring is complete.

---

## DAG Summary

```
milestone-01-codex-subagent   (root — no deps)
        |
        v
milestone-02-provider-dispatch
        |
        v
milestone-03-hetero-integration
```

**M01** delivers the Codex package in isolation — a reviewable, testable unit behind the `pkg/dispatch.Subagent` interface.
**M02** makes the controller provider-neutral — `ResolveProvider` reads config rather than hardcoding `"anthropic"`, Helm chart gains Codex knobs.
**M03** closes the loop — a real Project CR dispatches Claude planners + Codex executors across wave boundaries with correct failure semantics and observable cost telemetry for both providers.

## Plan of Record

Three milestones in a linear DAG — each a human-reviewable gate before the next level fans out:

### Milestone 01 — `milestone-01-codex-subagent` (root)

**Scope:** The Codex subagent package in isolation. Delivers `internal/subagent/codex/` — `client.go`, `run.go`, `doc.go`, `pricing.go`, `Dockerfile` — fully behind the `pkg/dispatch.Subagent` interface. Includes:
- Codex CLI invocation (`codex exec --ephemeral --skip-git-repo-check --ignore-user-config --json --sandbox workspace-write --output-schema`) with JSONL event-stream parsing.
- `vendorSentinel = "openai"` compile-time guard — rejects mismatched envelopes at startup.
- Usage normalization: `prompt_tokens → InputTokens`, `completion_tokens → OutputTokens`, `prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens → CacheReadTokens` (no `CacheCreationTokens` — OpenAI caching is automatic/marker-free), and per-model pricing table for `EstimatedCostCents` + `CacheSavingsCents`.
- Offline-safe tests; any live-API path gated by `OPENAI_API_KEY` env guard.
- Provider-firewall analyzer (`make verify-*`) passes — zero Codex imports in controller or manager.

**Gate:** Package compiles, tests pass, Dockerfile builds, pricing round-trips, sentinel test exercises the reject path.

---

### Milestone 02 — `milestone-02-provider-dispatch` (depends on M01)

**Scope:** Makes the controller provider-neutral. `ResolveProvider` in `dispatch_helpers.go` currently hardcodes `Vendor: "anthropic"` — this milestone replaces that with a precedence-chain walk identical to `resolveImage` (per-level → project default → Helm default). Also adds `SecretRefs.OpenAIAPIKey` so the dispatch Job mounts `OPENAI_API_KEY` from a declared K8s Secret into Codex-dispatched pods. Helm chart gains `images.codexSubagent.*` and the new secret-ref knob.

**Gate:** `ResolveProvider` unit tests cover both `"anthropic"` and `"openai"` resolution paths; backward-compat regression for projects with no per-level override; `make test` green; `make verify-*` green; `helm lint` passes.

---

### Milestone 03 — `milestone-03-hetero-integration` (depends on M02)

**Scope:** End-to-end proof — a real `Project` CR routes planners (milestone/phase/plan levels) to Claude and executors (task level) to Codex; the run completes across wave boundaries with correct failure semantics. Delivers:
- `examples/projects/hetero-codex/project.yaml` — the reference mixed-provider Project CR.
- `docs/hetero-providers.md` — operator runbook (Secret creation → `kubectl apply` → dashboard watch → failure interpretation).
- Integration test or recorded trace proving: Claude pods get `Vendor == "anthropic"` envelopes, Codex pods get `Vendor == "openai"` envelopes, a Codex wave failure blocks dependents-only (not independent later waves), and `tide_cost_cents_total` emits non-zero for both vendor label values.
- Helm override snippet for the dual-image, dual-secret install.

Fully behind `pkg/dispatch.Subagent`; `vendorSentinel = "openai"` compile-time guard; JSONL event-stream parsing; usage normalization (`prompt_tokens → InputTokens`, `completion_tokens → OutputTokens`, `prompt_tokens_details.cached_tokens → CacheReadTokens`, no `CacheCreationTokens` — OpenAI caching is automatic/marker-free); per-model pricing table for `EstimatedCostCents` + `CacheSavingsCents`. Offline-safe tests; live-API paths gated by `OPENAI_API_KEY` env.

**Gate:** package compiles, `make test` green, `make verify-*` passes (zero provider leakage), Dockerfile builds, pricing round-trips, sentinel test exercises the reject path.

---

### Milestone 02 — `milestone-02-provider-dispatch`
**DependsOn:** `milestone-01-codex-subagent`

Makes the controller provider-neutral. `ResolveProvider` in `dispatch_helpers.go` currently hardcodes `Vendor: "anthropic"` — this milestone rewrites it to walk the same precedence chain as `resolveImage` (per-level → project default → Helm default). Adds `SecretRefs.OpenAIAPIKey` so dispatch Jobs mount `OPENAI_API_KEY` from a declared K8s Secret. Helm chart gains `images.codexSubagent.*` and the new secret-ref operator knob.

**Gate:** `ResolveProvider` unit tests cover both `"anthropic"` and `"openai"` resolution paths + backward-compat regression; `make test` + `make verify-*` green; `helm lint` passes; `zz_generated.deepcopy.go` up-to-date.

---

### Milestone 03 — `milestone-03-hetero-integration`
**DependsOn:** `milestone-02-provider-dispatch`

End-to-end proof. A real `Project` CR routes planners (milestone/phase/plan levels) to Claude and executors (task level) to Codex across wave boundaries with correct failure semantics. Delivers `examples/projects/hetero-codex/project.yaml`, `docs/hetero-providers.md` (operator runbook), and an integration test or recorded trace proving: Claude pods receive `Vendor == "anthropic"` envelopes; Codex pods receive `Vendor == "openai"` envelopes; a Codex-wave task failure blocks only dependents (not independent later waves); `tide_cost_cents_total` emits non-zero for both vendor label values; dashboard cache-efficiency panel shows Codex cache-read savings.

**Gate:** example CR validates against live CRDs; integration test/trace documents all four observable properties; provider-firewall analyzer still clean.
